column89: Stage 4A checks settlement target races; contested-road timing is Stage 4B.;Stage 4C separates raw target value from opponent move viability using candidate ranking and opportunity cost.;3:1 port bonus follows refined rule: no existing 3:1 and useful surplus not already covered by 2:1 ports.;Stage 4B checks contested roads inside new-settlement paths.;Stage 4C uses opponent move viability to reduce false road-risk warnings for low-priority opponent paths.;Timing is path-position aware: reaching road 2 means affording roads 1 and 2 if both are unbuilt.;Stage 4D combines target risk, road risk, and opponent move viability into action-risk fields.;risk_adjusted_total_expected_own_turns adds a small penalty to the best continuation row for Excel sorting; raw totals remain unchanged.
